author: terencechain
content:
`on_block` is only called once parent is verified (gossip checks passing) 
im not sure where they say that in the spec, but there must be a place where the spec explicitly specify such

author: luca_zanolini
content: Oh ok, nice! All good then thanks

author: luca_zanolini
content: I'll try to see if I find it in the specs

author: luca_zanolini
content:
From what I can see, when a block declares its parent FULL and on_block's assert is_payload_verified(block.parent_root) fails, the block is simply not added to the store, and nothing in the spec re-invokes on_block when the parent payload later verifies. The gossip gate only requires the parent payload to have been seen, not verified.

Of course I'm not claiming this is a problem — maybe it can't even happen that we receive a block in slot N before we've finished verifying the payload revealed in slot N−1. I'm only saying that if it does, the spec doesn't say anywhere to run on_block again.

author: terencechain
content:
oh i think i understand the confusion now... In the gossip spec. `seen` means `verified`.  That's how we always implemented it even since phase 0: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/phase0/p2p-interface.md (search `seen`)

but i see the confusion now because gloas.. PTC.. it adds a whole new dimension of what `seen` could be 🙁
